title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 16 16

questions.question: Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for each item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. Ravi is planning a 2-day trek in a remote area. Plan A is cheaper but includes no medical cover, basic gear, and a large group with one guide. Plan B costs more, includes trained guides, safety gear, and emergency support. Ravi has asthma and the route has limited network coverage. Which option is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Ravi has a known health risk and the trek area is remote with weak connectivity. Option D directly reduces risk through trained guides, safety gear, and emergency support. Option A increases danger because help may not be available when needed. Option B is impractical because he can only trek once and it wastes money. Option C is the highest risk choice in a remote route.

questions.question: What comes next: 4, 13, 8, 17, 12, 21, 16, ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: Odd-position terms: 4, 8, 12, 16 4 + 4 = 8 8 + 4 = 12 12 + 4 = 16 So, odd-position terms increase by +4 each time. Even-position terms: 13, 17, 21, ? 13 + 4 = 17 17 + 4 = 21 So, next term = ? = 21 + 4 = 25

questions.question: The question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should foreign tourists be charged a higher entry fee than local residents at publicly funded museums? Argument I: Yes, because foreign tourists take more photographs, so they should pay more. Argument II: No, differential pricing can be fair if it helps subsidise locals and improves maintenance; but it should be reasonable and clearly communicated.
options: Neither argument I nor II is strong
Both arguments I and II are strong
questions.explanation: Argument I gives an unrelated reason (photographs) and does not justify pricing policy logically. Argument II directly discusses funding, access, and fairness with a balanced condition, so it is strong. So, only Argument II is strong.

questions.question: Group A: 108A135, 204E255, 244I305 Which of the following belongs to Group A?
questions.explanation: 108A135 = 108 * 5/4 = 135 204E255 = 204 * 5/4 = 255 244I305 = 244 * 5/4 = 305 Operation on letter = A, E, I, O, (Increasing order of vowel) Option (a): 284*3/2 = 426 (Not satisfied) Option (b): 184 * 7/4 = 322 (Not satisfied) Option (c): 324 * 5/4 = 405 (Satisfied) Option (d): 196 * 5/4 = 245, P is not a vowel (Not satisfied)

questions.question: Which number breaks the sequence? 8, 24, 48, 120, 164, 288, 360
In a coded inequality, if 'A # B' means A is greater than B and 'C @ B' means C is neither greater than nor equal to B, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: A # B = A > B C @ B = B > C A > B > C 1) A ≥ C [False] 2) A < C [False] 3) A ≤ C [false] 4) A > C [True]

questions.question: Choose the address that is the same as the one given below. 37/3A, Ramkrishna Nagar, Uttarakhand, Champawat, 262523
questions.explanation: (a): 252523 is not same (b): 222523 is not same (d): 262623 is not same Hence, option (c)

questions.explanation: Option a: 617 ≠ 671 Option b: 566 = 566 Option c: 468 ≠ 465 Option d: 725 ≠ 727 Hence, option 'b' is correct.

questions.question: Select the set in which the words are related in the same way as are the words of the following sets. (The word must be considered as meaningful English word and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word.) Broom - Sweeping Shovel - Digging
questions.explanation: Broom is used for sweeping. Shovel is used for digging. Similarly, Hoe is used for Ploughing.

questions.question: All the letters in the word 'PLANET' are arranged in alphabetical order (from left to right). How many letters are there in the English alphabetical series between the letter which is second from the left end and the one which is third from the right end in the new letter-cluster thus formed?
questions.explanation: The alphabetical order of "PLANET" is: A, E, L, N, P, T Second from the left end: E Third from the right end: N Letters between E and N are: F, G, H, I, J, K, L, M There are 8 letters.

questions.question: Three of the following four traids are alike in a certain way as they are formed by performing same mathematical operations among themselves and thus forms a group. Which traid does NOT belong to that group?

Select the option which has the set of letters & numbers which can sequentially replace the blanks of the given alpha-numeric series to complete the series. W __ 1 O __ N 2 Q __ W 5 __ O P __ 2 Q 7 __ 5 __ O P __ 2 Q 7 __ 5 1 O P N __ Q __
questions.explanation: The complete series is, W 5 1 O P N 2 Q 7 W 5 1 O P N 2 Q 7 W 5 1 O P N 2 Q 7 W 5 1 O P N 2 Q 7

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third letter cluster in the same way as the second letter-cluster is related to the first letter-cluster and the sixth letter-cluster is related to the fifth letter-cluster. RENAULT : UMVBOFS :: SKODA : ? :: CITROEN : OFPSUJD
questions.explanation: A (1)B (2)C (3)D (4)E (5)F (6)G (7)H (8)I (9)J (10)K (11)L (12)M (13) Z (26)Y (25)X (24)W (23)V (22)U (21)T (20)S (19)R (18)Q (17)P (16)O (15)N (14) RENAULT After reversing= TLUANER T+1L+1U+1A+1N+1E+1R+1RENAULT UMVBOFSUMVBOFS CITROEN After reversing = NEORTIC N+1E+1O+1R+1T+1I+1C+1CITROEN OFPSUJDOFPSUJD Similarly, SKODA After reversing = ADOKS A+1D+1O+1K+1S+1SKODA BEPLTBEPLT Thus, the answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number, the sixth number is related to the fifth number. 9 : 49 :: 15 : ? :: 4 : 4
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 9 - 2 = 7 and 7*7 = 49 4 - 2 = 2 and 2*2 = 4 Similarly, 15 - 2 = 13 and 13*13 = 169

questions.question: Four of the following five are somehow similar and therefore create a group. Which is not related to this group? Fear, Surprise, Anger, Excitement, Commitment
questions.explanation: Fear, Surprise, Anger and Excitement are emotions but commitment is an action.

questions.question: In the following question, a matrix consist of 3 rows and 3 columns. Select the number which can be placed at the sign of question mark (?) from the given alternatives. 13426 17?8 234221
questions.explanation: If we consider numbers across rows, then the pattern is: For the 1st row: 13 - 6 = 7 and 7*number present in row 1-column 3 = 7*6 = 42 For the 3rd row: 23 - 21 = 2 and 2* number present in row 3-column 3 = 2*21 = 42 Similarly, For the 2nd row: 17 - 8 = 9 and 9* number present in row 2-column 3 = 9*8 = 72

questions.question: 'P ! Q' means 'P is the mother of Q'. 'P @ Q' means 'P is the sister of Q'. 'P $ Q' means 'P is the father of Q'. 'P % Q' means 'P is the child of Q'. 'P & Q' means 'P is the son of Q'. If A $ J & B % G ! H @ N & U, then how is U related to A?
questions.explanation: It is given that: 'P ! Q' means 'P is the mother of Q'. 'P @ Q' means 'P is the sister of Q'. 'P $ Q' means 'P is the father of Q'. 'P % Q' means 'P is the child of Q'. 'P & Q' means 'P is the son of Q'. After decoding the expression, 'A $ J & B % G ! H @ N & U' we get: A is the father of J. J is the son of B. B is the child of G. G is the mother of H. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21578/image004.jpg) H is the sister of N. N is the son of U. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21578/image005.jpg) Thus, U is the father-in-law of A.

questions.question: Misha's sister's father is the father-in-law of Tanya's husband's mother. How is Tanya related to Misha?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24695/image010.jpg) Misha's sister's father is the father-in-law of Tanya's husband's mother. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24695/image011.jpg) So, Tanya is the daughter-in-law of Misha's brother.

How many alphabet positions will not change if the letters in the word "RAILWAY" are placed from left to right in alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: RAILWAY AAILRWY So, the position of the four letters will not change. Thus, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which team won the men's title at the inaugural Hockey India Masters Cup 2025 held in Chennai?
questions.explanation: The Hockey Unit of Tamil Nadu won the men's title at the first edition of the Hockey India Masters Cup 2025, defeating Hockey Maharashtra 5-0 in the final match held in Chennai. Tamil Nadu's Masters team defeated Maharashtra in the final to become the inaugural champions of the men's Masters Cup Further Insights Masters Cup featured veteran players aged 40 and above in the men's category. tournament was held at the Mayor Radhakrishnan Hockey Stadium in Chennai from 18-27 June 2025. Hockey Association of Odisha won the women's title in the same event. Hockey Chandigarh secured the men's bronze medal at the Masters Cup.

questions.question: During the Non-Cooperation Movement thousands of Indians were put in jail. Gandhiji himself was arrested in March _______
questions.explanation: During the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-1922), thousands of Indians were imprisoned for participating in various forms of non-violent protest and civil disobedience. Mahatma Gandhi himself was arrested on March 10, 1922, following the Chauri Chaura incident (February 1922) where a violent mob set fire to a police station, leading to the deaths of several policemen. Gandhi, deeply disturbed by this violence, called off the Non-Cooperation Movement. He was subsequently arrested, tried for sedition, and sentenced to six years in prison, though he was released in February 1924 on medical grounds. Explanation of Other Options: (a) 1918: This predates the Non-Cooperation Movement. In 1918, Gandhi was involved in movements like the Kheda Satyagraha and Ahmedabad mill strike. (b) 1924: Gandhi was released from prison in February 1924. He was not arrested in March of this year in the context of the Non-Cooperation Movement (which had already been called off). (d) 1920: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in 1920 (formally in August/September). While many were arrested during its course, Gandhi's arrest that led to his imprisonment occurred in March 1922. Further insights: The Non-Cooperation Movement was a significant phase in the Indian independence struggle, launched by Mahatma Gandhi and the Indian National Congress. It involved boycotting British goods, institutions (schools, colleges, law courts), and government services, and promoting Swadeshi (indigenous) goods and self-reliance. The Khilafat Movement, which aimed to restore the Caliph of Turkey, also merged with the Non-Cooperation Movement, fostering Hindu-Muslim unity. The Chauri Chaura incident was a critical turning point, leading Gandhi to suspend the movement as he felt the people were not yet ready for sustained non-violent struggle. Gandhi's trial in March 1922 was a notable event where he accepted full responsibility for the events and made a powerful statement.

questions.question: Which of the following terms refers to increase in concentration of the toxicant at successive trophic levels?
questions.explanation: Biomagnification refers to increase in concentration of the toxicant at successive trophic levels. This happens because a toxic substance accumulated by an organism cannot be metabolised or excreted, and is thus passed on to the next higher trophic level. Eutrophication is the natural aging of a lake by nutrient enrichment of its water. Biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) represents the amount of oxygen consumed by bacteria and other microorganisms while they decompose organic matter under aerobic (oxygen is present) conditions at a specified temperature.

questions.question: In which of the following country Jhumming is known as 'Ladang'?
questions.explanation: Jhumming, which is a form of shifting cultivation or slash-and-burn agriculture, is known as 'Ladang' in (b) Indonesia and also in Malaysia. This agricultural practice involves clearing a piece of land by cutting down and burning the vegetation, cultivating crops for a few years, and then abandoning the land to allow it to regenerate while the farmers move to a new plot. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Brazil: In Brazil, shifting cultivation is known as 'Roca'. (c) Vietnam: In Vietnam, it is known as 'Ray'. (d) Venezuela: In Venezuela, it is known as 'Conuco'. Further insights: Shifting cultivation has different local names across the world and even within different regions of India. In Northeast India, it is commonly known as Jhum or Jhumming. Other international names include 'Milpa' in Central America and Mexico, and 'Masole' in Central Africa. While a traditional practice, it is often criticized for causing deforestation and soil erosion, though traditional systems sometimes incorporate long fallow periods for forest regeneration.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about some Padma Awardees in 2025: 1. P. R. Sreejesh, the Indian field hockey goalkeeper, was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 2025. 2. Shekhar Kapur, the filmmaker, received the Padma Shri in 2025. 3. M. T. Vasudevan Nair, the Malayalam writer, was awarded the Padma Vibhushan posthumously in 2025. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: P. R. Sreejesh was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 2025. The former Indian hockey team goalkeeper was one of 19 recipients of the Padma Bhushan for his contributions to sports. Shekhar Kapur received the Padma Bhushan, not Padma Shri. The renowned filmmaker received the Padma Bhushan for his work in Art. M. T. Vasudevan Nair was given the Padma Vibhushan posthumously in 2025. The acclaimed writer was one of seven individuals to receive the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian award, which was accepted by his wife on his behalf.

questions.question: Which of the following are Non-tax revenue receipts of the Government of India? I. Profits from Public sector units II. Investment dividends III. Cess IV. Grants from abroad Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
options: I, II, III and IV only
questions.explanation: Government receipts are divided into two groups - Revenue Receipts and Capital Receipts . Revenue receipts neither create any liability nor cause any reduction in the assets of the government. These receipts are classified into (a) Tax Revenue and (b) Non-tax Revenue. Tax Revenue : A tax is a legal compulsory payment by the people and firms to the government of a country without reference to any direct benefit in return. It is imposed on the people by the government. A government collects revenue from various taxes like income tax, sales tax, service tax, excise duty, customs duty, cess and surcharge. The incomes accruing to government from sources other than taxes are non-tax revenues. E.g. electricity bills, postal stamps, toll, passport fees, court fee, fines and penalties, Interest receipts, investment dividends, profits of public sector undertakings, grants, interest and dividend on government investment. Source: NIOS

questions.question: Consider the following statements on Vedic material culture: I. In the Rigvedic context, nishka denotes primarily a golden ornament rather than a coin of fixed face value. II. Painted Grey Ware (PGW) pottery is typically associated with early Rigvedic settlements in the Sapta-Sindhu region. Which of the above is/are incorrect?
options: Both I and II are incorrect
Both I and II are correct
questions.explanation: Nishka in early Vedic references is a gold ornament or neck torc given as a gift; it is not a coined money of fixed denomination. PGW is associated with the later Vedic/early Iron Age horizon, especially in the GangaYamuna doab, not the early Rigvedic Sapta-Sindhu zone. Further Insights: - PGW horizon broadly ranges c. 1200-600 BCE; Northern Black Polished Ware (NBPW) follows (c. 700200 BCE peak). - Rigvedic economy was largely pastoral-agricultural; cattle wealth was a key value measure. - Terms like satamana and krishnala relate to weights; coined money emerges clearly much later. - The later Vedic phase (with iron use-shyamaayas) is linked to eastern expansion into the middle Ganga plains. - The Sarasvati in the Rigveda is lauded as a mighty river; its identification with Ghaggar-Hakra is a matter of scholarly debate.

questions.question: India achieved its best-ever Asian Games tally at Hangzhou (held in 2023). What was the total medal count?
questions.explanation: India finished with 107 medals at Hangzhou (postponed 2022 edition held in 2023). This included 28 gold, 38 silver, and 41 bronze medals. Further Insights: - India ranked 4th in the overall medal table at Hangzhou. - Athletics and shooting were major contributors to the medal tally. - The Asian Games were postponed due to the pandemic and held in 2023 in Hangzhou, China. - India's previous best was 70 medals at JakartaPalembang 2018.

questions.question: Which five year plan had the objective of 'Garibi Hatao' and 'attainment of self reliance'?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-1979) was formulated with the two primary objectives of 'Garibi Hatao' (poverty eradication) and the 'attainment of self-reliance'. The slogan 'Garibi Hatao' was famously used by the then Prime Minister Indira Gandhi during her election campaign and was subsequently incorporated as a key goal of this plan. Explanation of Other Options: Fourth five year plan (1969-74): Focused on 'growth with stability' and 'progressive achievement of self-reliance'. Sixth five year plan (1980-85): Also focused heavily on poverty alleviation by strengthening the socio-economic infrastructure in rural areas, but the 'Garibi Hatao' slogan was the defining feature of the Fifth Plan. Third five year plan (1961-66): Aimed to establish a self-reliant and self-generating economy but was largely unsuccessful due to wars and drought. Further insights: The Fifth Plan was prepared by the planner D.P. Dhar. It was notable for being terminated one year early, in 1978, by the Janata Party government which came to power in 1977.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Attorney General of India is NOT TRUE?
options: He must be a member of either House of Parliament.
He is appointed by the President of India.
He is the highest law officer in the country.
He has the right to speak in the proceedings of either House of Parliament.
questions.explanation: The statement that is NOT TRUE is (a) "He must be a member of either House of Parliament." The Attorney General of India is not a member of Parliament. One of the qualifications for the post is to be qualified to be appointed as a judge of the Supreme Court, but this does not require membership in Parliament. Explanation of Other Options: (b) is true: According to Article 76 of the Constitution, the President appoints the Attorney General. (c) is true: The Attorney General is the highest law officer in India and the chief legal advisor to the Government of India. (d) is true: The Attorney General has the right to speak and take part in the proceedings of both Houses of Parliament (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha), any joint sitting, and any committee of Parliament of which he may be named a member, but he does not have the right to vote.

questions.question: With reference to writs and judicial powers, consider the following: 1) High Courts can issue writs not only for Fundamental Rights but also "for any other purpose" under Article 226. 2) The Supreme Court issues writs primarily for enforcement of Fundamental Rights under Article 32. 3) Quo warranto tests a person's legal authority to hold a public office. 4) Parliament cannot empower any other court to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Article 226 confers a wider writ jurisdiction on High Courts (FRs and other legal rights). Statement 2 is correct: Article 32 is for FR enforcement in the Supreme Court. Statement 3 is correct: Quo warranto challenges the lawful claim to a public office. Statement 4 is incorrect: Article 32(3) permits Parliament to empower other courts to exercise similar powers. Further Insights: - Article 141: the law declared by the Supreme Court is binding on all courts in India. - Habeas corpus lies to test unlawful detention; certiorari corrects jurisdictional errors of lower courts/tribunals; prohibition prevents them from exceeding jurisdiction. - High Courts have superintendence over subordinate courts/tribunals under Article 227 (excluding armed forces tribunals). - Article 32 is itself a Fundamental Right; Article 226 is a constitutional remedy but not a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: Evaluate the following statements about Manipuri dance. Statement 1. Ras Lila is a central repertoire of Manipuri, marked by rounded, soft movements; dancers typically do not emphasize loud ankle bells. Statement 2. Manipuri is characterized by forceful hip sways and stamped footwork derived from Kalaripayattu training.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Manipuri emphasizes subtle, rounded movements with restrained use of ghungroo. Statement 2 is incorrect; Kalaripayattu is a Kerala martial art and its forceful stamps are not characteristic of Manipuri. Further Insights: - Pung Cholom (drum dance) and Kartal Cholom are distinctive Manipuri forms. - Costumes: Potloi (stiff cylindrical skirt) for female Ras Lila; dhotis and turbans for males. - Manipuri is a Vaishnavite-influenced classical dance from Manipur, often themed on Krishna-Radha devotion. - Key instruments: pung (drum), flute, cymbals; singing is integral to performances.

questions.question: Which of the following folk dance forms does NOT belong to the state of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Chappeli (or Chhapeli) is a popular folk dance that does NOT belong to Rajasthan. It is primarily associated with the state of Uttarakhand and is also performed in parts of Himachal Pradesh. It is a dance of romance, performed by couples with a mirror in one hand and a colorful handkerchief in the other. Explanation of Other Options: Ghoomar: This is a famous traditional folk dance of Rajasthan, performed by women of the Bhil tribe and later adopted by other Rajasthani communities. It involves graceful swirling movements. Gawri: This is a well-known dance-drama performed by the Bhil tribe in the Mewar region of Rajasthan. It is a month-long festival that re-enacts mythological and historical events. Kalbelia: This is a sensuous dance performed by the women of the Kalbelia (snake charmer) community of Rajasthan. Its movements often mimic those of a serpent. It is recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity. Further insights: Rajasthan has a very rich and diverse culture of folk dances, including others like Bhavai, Terah Taali, and Kachchhi Ghodi. Folk dances are an integral part of understanding the cultural fabric of different Indian states.

questions.question: Which statement about work done by static friction is correct?
options: For a rigid cylinder rolling without slipping down a rough incline, static friction does zero net work in the ground frame.
For a car accelerating forward on a horizontal road without slipping, static friction does positive work on the car in the ground frame.
For a spool pulled on a rough floor without slipping, static friction always does negative work.
For a block pulled at constant speed on a rough surface, static friction does positive work.
questions.explanation: For pure rolling without slipping, the instantaneous point of contact has zero velocity relative to the ground, so static friction does zero instantaneous power and hence zero net work, while gravity supplies the energy that is partitioned between translation and rotation. Thus, "For a rigid cylinder rolling without slipping down a rough incline, static friction does zero net work" is correct. Other options: - "B. For a car accelerating forward on a horizontal road without slipping, static friction does positive work on the car in the ground frame." Incorrect; static friction does no work because the point of contact is instantaneously at rest; the engine's internal forces increase the car's kinetic energy. - "C. For a spool pulled on a rough floor without slipping, static friction always does negative work." Incorrect; with no slipping, static friction's work is zero; with a moving surface (e.g., belt) it can be positive or negative depending on kinematics. - "D. For a block pulled at constant speed on a rough surface, static friction does positive work." Incorrect; the force opposing motion is kinetic friction (not static) and it does negative work. Further Insights: - True (actual) work by a contact force equals F·v of the point of application; for rolling contact on a stationary surface, that v is zero. - Translational work - energy (pseudo-work) uses F_net acting on the center of mass times CM displacement; it differs from actual work at the point of contact. - On a moving belt (no slip), static friction can do positive work on an object, spinning it up into rolling at belt speed. - Rolling friction (due to deformations) is non-conservative and can dissipate energy; it is distinct from ideal static friction.

questions.question: The fall in the sea surface temperature in the central and western Pacific Ocean below average is known as
questions.explanation: La Nina occurs when the normal circulation over the southern Pacific Ocean strengthens as a result of the subtropical high developing over the southern Pacific Ocean. This leads to strong south east trade winds blowing away from this region which drags much of the warm water westward, resulting in fall in the sea surface temperature in the central and western Pacific Ocean. Explanation of Other Options: (a) El Nio: This is the warm phase of ENSO, characterized by the warming of the sea surface temperature above average in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific Ocean. (c) IOD (Indian Ocean Dipole): This is a climate pattern related to the sea surface temperature difference between the western and eastern parts of the Indian Ocean, not the Pacific Ocean. (d) Walker Circulation: This is the large-scale atmospheric circulation pattern over the tropical Pacific Ocean. It is driven by the temperature and pressure difference between the western and eastern Pacific. La Nia represents an intensification of the normal Walker Circulation, while El Nio represents a weakening or reversal of it. Walker Circulation is the atmospheric process, while La Nia/El Nio are the oceanic states that define the ENSO cycle. Further insights: La Nia has significant impacts on global weather patterns, often bringing opposite effects to those of El Nio. For example, it can lead to increased rainfall in regions like Southeast Asia and Australia, and drier conditions in parts of North and South America. The ENSO cycle (alternating between El Nio, La Nia, and neutral conditions) is one of the most important drivers of year-to-year climate variability worldwide.

questions.question: In Census 2011, a "main worker" is a person who worked for at least ____ days during the reference year.
questions.explanation: Census 2011 defines a "main worker" as a person who worked for at least 183 days (six months) or more in the reference year; those who worked for less than 183 days are classified as marginal workers. This threshold helps distinguish stable employment from seasonal or marginal engagement. Further Insights: - Work participation rate (WPR) is computed separately for main and marginal workers to capture labor-force structure. - Census 2011 also classifies workers by four industrial categories: cultivators, agricultural laborers, household industry workers, and other workers.

questions.question: According to government data and policy reviews, what is India's current global rank in terms of the size of its domestic aviation market?
questions.explanation: According to recent government data and aviation policy reports, India has emerged as the third-largest domestic aviation market in the world, behind only the United States and China. India's air travel sector - particularly flights within the country - has grown rapidly, with passenger numbers and airline capacity increasing significantly. As a result, India now ranks third globally in terms of the size of its domestic aviation market. Further Insights India's domestic aviation market has been growing strongly due to rising incomes and expanding connectivity. The number of airports in India has more than doubled over the past decade, aiding growth. UDAN (Regional Connectivity Scheme) has contributed to increased air traffic in tier-2 and tier-3 cities. India's domestic traffic growth rate often outpaces many other major aviation markets. Airlines like IndiGo dominate India's domestic segment with a significant market share.

questions.question: Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the code given below the lists: A. Article 22i. Protection in respect of conviction for offences B. Article 20ii. Freedom for payment of taxes for promotion of any religion C. Article 27iii. Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour D. Article 23iv. Protection against arrest and detection in certain cases
questions.explanation: A. Article 22i. Protection against arrest and detection in certain cases B. Article 20ii. Protection in respect of conviction for offences C. Article 27iii. Freedom for payment of taxes for promotion of any religion D. Article 23iv. Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana' (PMSGMBY): i) By March 10, 2025, the scheme achieved a historic milestone by solarising a total of 10 lakh (1 million) households across India. ii) Each solar power plant installed under this initiative helps reduce carbon emissions by an amount equivalent to planting 100 trees. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is Correct Under PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana, the government announced that by early March 2025, more than 10 lakh households had been solarised. The scheme aims to install rooftop solar systems in 1 crore households, and crossing the 10-lakh milestone was highlighted as a major achievement. Statement (ii) is Correct Government communications stated that each rooftop solar installation helps reduce carbon emissions equivalent to planting around 100 trees annually. Rooftop solar replaces fossil-fuel electricity, reducing greenhouse gas emissions - and the environmental benefit was simplified in public messaging using the "100 trees" comparison. Further Insights The scheme targets 1 crore households nationwide. Beneficiaries can receive subsidies up to ₹78,000 for rooftop solar installation. The scheme supports India's net-zero by 2070 climate goal. Rooftop solar helps reduce electricity bills for households. It promotes decentralised renewable energy generation.

questions.question: Usually which of the following are added to foods containing fats and oil to prevent them from becoming rancid?
questions.explanation: To prevent foods containing fats and oils from becoming rancid, (a) Antioxidants are usually added. Rancidity is the process of oxidation or hydrolysis of fats and oils, leading to an unpleasant odor and taste. Antioxidants are substances that inhibit or slow down the process of oxidation. They work by sacrificing themselves to be oxidized by free radicals, thereby protecting the fats and oils. Explanation of Other Options: (b) Antimicrobials: These are agents that kill or inhibit the growth of microorganisms like bacteria and fungi. They are used as preservatives to prevent spoilage due to microbial contamination, which is different from oxidative rancidity. (c) Anticoagulants: These are substances that prevent blood from clotting. They are used medically (e.g., heparin, warfarin) and have no role in preventing food rancidity. (d) Antigens: These are substances (often proteins or polysaccharides) that can trigger an immune response in the body. They are not used as food additives to prevent rancidity. Further insights: Common antioxidants used in the food industry include Butylated Hydroxyanisole (BHA), Butylated Hydroxytoluene (BHT), and Propyl Gallate. Natural antioxidants like Vitamin E (tocopherols) and Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) are also used. Another method to prevent rancidity is to package foods in an inert atmosphere (like nitrogen) to exclude oxygen.

questions.question: Which of the following type of money supply is the most liquid and easiest for transactions?
questions.explanation: Among the measures of money supply in India, (a) M1 is the most liquid and easiest for transactions. It is also known as "narrow money." Liquidity refers to the ease with which an asset can be converted into cash to be used for transactions. M1 consists of: Currency with the Public (coins and paper notes) Demand Deposits of commercial banks (money in current and savings accounts that can be withdrawn on demand) 'Other' Deposits with the RBI. Since all components of M1 are either cash itself or can be converted to cash instantly on demand, it is the most liquid measure. Explanation of Other Options: (b) M2: M2 = M1 + Savings deposits with Post Office savings banks. Less liquid than M1 because post office savings are not as easily transactable as bank demand deposits. (c) M3: M3 = M1 + Net Time Deposits of the commercial banks. Time deposits (like Fixed Deposits) have a fixed maturity period and cannot be withdrawn on demand without penalty, making them less liquid than demand deposits. M3 is known as "broad money." (d) M4: M4 = M3 + All deposits with post office savings banks (excluding National Savings Certificates). This is the least liquid of the four measures. Further insights: The measures of money supply are arranged in decreasing order of liquidity: M1 > M2 > M3 > M4. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) uses these measures to monitor the money supply in the economy and formulate its monetary policy.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the Great Stupa at Sanchi: I.It was originally commissioned by the Mauryan emperor Ashoka. II.It is famous for its four ornamental gateways (toranas) which depict various Jataka tales. III.The main purpose of the stupa was to serve as a prayer hall for Buddhist monks. Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements I and II are correct. Statement I is correct: The Great Stupa at Sanchi was originally commissioned in the 3rd century BCE by the Mauryan emperor Ashoka the Great to enshrine the relics of the Buddha. Statement II is correct: The stupa is renowned for its four intricately carved gateways, or toranas, which were added later in the 1st century BCE. These gateways depict scenes from the life of the Buddha and various Jataka tales (stories of the Buddha's previous lives). Explanation of Incorrect Statement: Statement III is incorrect: The main purpose of a stupa is to be a reliquary mound, a sacred structure containing Buddhist relics. It is an object of veneration, around which devotees circumambulate. The prayer hall for Buddhist monks is called a Chaitya.

questions.question: In the context of Hindustani Classical Music, which of the following pairs of Gharanas and their famous exponents is correctly matched?
options: Agra Gharana - Bade Ghulam Ali Khan
questions.explanation: Pt. Bhimsen Joshi was one of the greatest exponents of the Kirana Gharana. Faiyaz Khan belonged to the Agra Gharana The Patiala Gharana is associated with vocalists like Ustad Bade Ghulam Ali Khan Vishnu Digambar Paluskar was associated with the Gwalior tradition Further Insights Gwalior Gharana is considered the oldest Hindustani gharana. Patiala Gharana is famous for bold, fast, and ornamented singing. Agra Gharana evolved from Dhrupad tradition. Vishnu Digambar Paluskar played a key role in popularizing classical music among masses.

questions.question: The term "Glide" is a specific technique primarily associated with which of the following sports?
questions.explanation: The Glide technique in Shot Put involves the athlete gliding backward across the throwing circle before releasing the shot. Further insights · Discus Throw - grip, wind-up, rotation, release, follow-through · Javelin Throw - approach, carry, crossover, delivery stride, release · Shot Put - glide, spin, power position, delivery, reverse · Long Jump - approach run, take-off, flight, hang technique, landing

questions.question: The Deccan Plateau is primarily composed of which type of rock?
questions.explanation: The Deccan Plateau is a large volcanic plateau composed primarily of basaltic lava flows from the Cretaceous period, covering about 500,000 km. The Deccan Plateau's primary rock type is basalt, a type of extrusive igneous rock formed from lava flows. This extensive volcanic activity, which occurred millions of years ago, created the large igneous province known as the Deccan Traps that covers much of the plateau. While basalt is the most common rock, granite is also present, especially in older rock formations.

questions.question: Saket has his final year exam at XYZ Engineering College. He left his home and travelled at a certain speed so that he would reach the college on time. After travelling half the distance, he realised that he left his admit card at home. He turned back immediately and increased his speed by 50% and reached his home. As compared to his original speed, by what percent should Saket increase his speed so that he still reaches the college on time?
questions.explanation: Suppose the distance between home and college is 12 km and the speed is 2 kmph. It would take 6 hrs to reach college. When he reaches half way, he has already spent 3 hours. He turns back at a speed of 3 kmph and takes 6/3 = 2 hours to reach home. Now, he has only 1 hour to reach college. So the speed must be 12 kmph. Thus the increase in speed is (12-2)/2 X 100 = 500%.

questions.question: Out of 25,000 registered voters, 80% voted in an election. If 3% of the votes cast were invalid and the remaining were shared equally by two loser candidates and the winner candidate gets 75% of valid votes, how many votes did each loser candidate receive?
questions.explanation: Total registered voters = 25000 Voters who cast their votes = 80% of 25000 = 20000 Valid votes = (100 - 3)% of 20000 = 19400 Votes received by each candidate = ½ (25% of 19400) = 2425

questions.question: θ and Φ are the two acute angles and sum of θ and Φ is also an acute angle. If tan(2θ + 40°) - cot(2Φ - 20°) = 0, then what is the value of (θ + Φ)?
If p = Sin A + Cos A and q = Sin A - Cos A, then what is the value of p2 - 1_________1 - q2
questions.explanation: p = SinA + CosA (Given) p2 = (SinA + CosA)2 = Sin2A + Cos2A + 2SinACosA = 1 + Sin2A q = SinA - CosA q2 = (SinA - CosA)2 = Sin2A + Cos2A - 2SinACosA = 1 - Sin2A Now the value of expression = (p2 - 1)/(1 - q2) = (1 + Sin2A - 1)/(1 - 1 + Sin2A) => Sin2A/Sin2A = 1

questions.question: A sum of Rs. (2P - 600) at 20% per annum compounded annually for two years is amounted Rs. (3P - 1080), then what is the value of P?
A completes 3/5​ of a job in 9 days, while B complete 2/3 of the same job in 8 days. If A and B work together, in how many days will they complete the entire job?
questions.explanation: One day work of A = (3/5)/9 = 1/15 One day work of B = (2/3)/8 = 1/12 One day work of A and B together = (1/15) + (1/12) = 3/20 Time taken by A and B together = 20/3 = 6(2/3) days

questions.question: Each of the questions below consists of a question and two statements numbered I and II given below it. You have to decide whether the data provided in the statements are sufficient to answer the question. Read both the statements and give answer: Find the profit received by a shopkeeper by selling a watch for Rs.2484? Statement I: Marked up amount of the watch is Rs.360. Statement II: Profit percent of the shopkeeper is 3.5%.
options: Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question but the statement II alone is not sufficient.
Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question but the statement I alone is not sufficient.
Both statements I and II together are needed to answer the question.
Either statement I alone or statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question.
questions.explanation: Let CP of the watch = 'x' rupees SP of the watch = Rs.2484 From statement I alone: Marked up amount of the watch = Rs.360 So, marked price of the watch = (x + 360) rupees Statement I alone is not sufficient to answer the question. From statement II alone: Profit per cent of the shopkeeper = 3.5% So, CP of the watch = 2484 * (100/103.5) = Rs.2400 Profit received by the shopkeeper = 2484 - 2400 = Rs.84 Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question. Hence, statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question but the statement I alone is not sufficient.

questions.question: A cuboidal solid has length of 30 cm, breadth of 20 cm and height of 12 cm. A diagonal plane cuts the box from one top edge to the opposite bottom edge, dividing it into two equal triangular prism - shaped sections. Find the volume of one of the triangular prism - shaped sections.
questions.explanation: L = 30 cm, B = 20 cm, H = 12 cm Volume of triangular prism = (½ * L * B) * H = ½ * 30 * 20 * 12 = 3600 cm3

questions.question: A teacher is demonstrating the concept of triangle using some plastic sticks. Now, she wants her students to create triangles with sides having integral values and semi-perimeter to be 7. A student came and drew two such triangles. But, according to the teacher, more number of such triangles can be drawn. How many such additional triangles can be drawn?
questions.explanation: Let the sides of the triangle be a, b and c. (a + b + c)/2 = 7 a + b + c = 14 In a triangle, sum of any two sides is greater than the third side. Thus, number of possible combination of triangles with length of sides is: 6, 4, 4 5, 5, 4 5, 6, 3 6, 6, 2 As student has already drawn two such triangles, so 2 more additional triangles can be drawn.

questions.question: The angles of elevation of a pole from the foot of two trees are 45° and 60°. The trees are on opposite side of the pole. If the distance between the trees is 150 m, then find the height of the pole.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28978/image004.png) In ∆XYW- tan60° = XW/WY XW = WY√3 ------(1) In ∆XWZ - tan45° = XW/WZ XW = WZ -----(2) From equation (1) and (2) - WY√3 = WZ WZ = (150 - WZ)*√3 WZ = 150√3/(1 + √3) = 75√3(√3 - 1) XW = 75√3(√3 - 1) m

questions.question: (3a - 4b)3 is equal to:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19398/image082.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19398/image083.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19398/image084.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19398/image085.png)

questions.question: Kiran purchased Highlighter and pencil from a shop for a total sum of Rs. 180. From the following data, what is the value of 'X'? ItemsPrice per unit (In Rs.)Quantity Highlighters10X Pencils206
questions.explanation: Total cost price of Pencils = 20 * 6 = Rs. 120 Total cost price of Highlighters = 180 - 120 = Rs. 60 Quantity of highlighters = 60/10 = 6 X = 6

questions.question: To determine the nature of Y (odd or even), consider the following statements: Statement I: X is odd Statement II: X (X + Y) is odd
options: Even together, statements are not sufficient
questions.explanation: I: X is odd [Not sufficient] II: X (X + Y) is odd A product is odd only if both factors are odd. X is odd So, (X + Y) is odd Now, if X is odd and X + Y is odd, then: odd + Y = odd Y must be even [Sufficient]

questions.question: A circular park is surrounded by a path. The total area of the park along with path is 1256 m2 and the park area is 706.5 m2. If the side of a square is equal to the difference between the width of the path and the radius of the circle, then find the perimeter of the square. (Take π = 3.14)
questions.explanation: Let the width of the path = X m Radius of circle = R m Area of the park = 3.14 * R2 = 706.5 R2 = 225 R = 15 m Area of the path + park = 3.14 * (R + X)2 = 1256 (15 + X)2 = 400 15 + X = 20 X = 5 Side of square = 15 - 5 = 10 m Perimeter of the square = 4*10 = 40 m

questions.question: If m = √10 + 3, then the value of m3 + 1_________m3 is:
The value of A for which the expression 2x3 + x2 - 6x + A will be divisible by (x - 3) is:
questions.explanation: Let the given expression is divisible by (x - 3), then by putting x = 3 in the expression, will give 0 as remainder. So, 2 * (3)3 + 32 - 18 + A = 0 54 + 9 - 18 + A = 0 A = -45

questions.question: Let X be the simple interest on principal Y, and Z be the simple interest on principal X, both at the same rate (r%) and for the same time (t years). What is the relation among X, Y, and Z?
questions.explanation: Define r = rate of interest (in %) and t = time (in years). X = (Y·r·t)/100 => r·t/100 = X/Y Z = (X·r·t)/100 => r·t/100 = Z/X Equate the two expressions for r·t/100: X/Y = Z/X Cross-multiply: X2 = Y·Z

questions.question: Which of the following fraction is in ascending order?
What is the value of (cos240° + cos250°)/(sec230° - 1)?
Find the length of the part of the straight line 12x + 35y = 420 intercepted between the axes.
questions.explanation: Straight line => 12x + 35y = 420 12x/420 + 35y/420 = 1 x/35 + y/12 = 1 Comparing with x/a + y/b = 1 a = 35, b = 12 Length of the line segment = √(a2 + b2) = √(352 + 122) = √(1225 + 144) = √1369 = 37 units

questions.question: When 'n' is subtracted from the numbers 9, 15 and 27, the remainders are in continued proportion. What is the value of 'n'?
questions.explanation: According to the question, (9-N), (15-N) and (27-N) are in continued proportion. Hence, (15-n)/(9-n)=(27-n)/(15-n) => n2 - 30n + 225 = n2 - 36n + 243 => 6n = 18 => n = 3

questions.question: 25 men completely build a wall working for 40 days. After working for 24 days 35 more men joined the workers. Now remaining portion of the wall will be built in how many days?
questions.explanation: Total work = 25 x 40 units Let after 35 more men, the remaining portion of the wall will be built in Y days. Therefore, Total work = 25 x 24 + (25 + 35) x Y units Now, 25 x 40 = 25 x 24 + 60 x Y Y = 25 x 16/60 = 20/3 = 6(2/3) days

questions.question: A cuboidal water tank has a length of 25 m, breadth of 20 m and a height of 60 m. It is filled with water up to a height of 35 m. If four identical cubical stones, each with a side of 4 m, are placed into the tank and become completely submerged, then find the rise in the water level in the tank.
questions.explanation: Side of cubical stones = 4 m Volume of four cubical stones = 4 * 43 = 256 m3 Area of base of cuboidal tank = 25 * 20 = 500 m3 Rise in the water level in the tank = 256/500 = 0.512 m = 51.2 cm

questions.question: Select the option that correctly replaces the underlined phrase in the following sentence: She recovered from the illness after taking complete rest and proper medication.
questions.explanation: The phrase "got over" means to recover from an illness, setback, or difficult situation, which correctly fits the sentence. Got over - Correct, as it means to recover from an illness or hardship (recovering from illness). Got along - Means to have a good relationship with someone (e.g., They got along well with each other). Got up - Means to rise from a sitting or lying position (e.g., He got up early in the morning). Got away - Means to escape or leave a place (e.g., The thief got away before the police arrived). Thus, the correct substitution is "got over." Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Identify the segment that contains an error. Much efforts / have been made / to improve the system / over the past year.
questions.explanation: "Efforts" is a plural countable noun. "Much" is used with uncountable nouns. The correct word here that should be used here is "many". It is used with the countable nouns. Correct sentence: Many efforts have been made to improve the system over the past year. So, there is an error in the part mentioned in option (a). Hence, option (a) is the answer. Note: Countable and uncountable nouns: 1. Countable nouns are the class of nouns that can be counted, for example, 'table' - one table, two tables, three tables, etc. 2. Uncountable nouns are the nouns that cannot be counted or usually are not counted, for example, light, water, milk, information, furniture, etc. They do not generally have a plural form and can't be preceded by 'a' or 'an'. Many abstract nouns are typically uncountable. Some uncountable nouns can be used in the plural as well, depending on the meaning or context of the word.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. A good many of those doctors don't approve for unorthodox medicine.
questions.explanation: The sentence contains a prepositional error. 'Approve' suggests agreement or consent and should be used alone or followed by the preposition 'of', not 'for'. For example: The team owner approved the manager's request. -> You usually get someone to approve your idea/plan/project before you start implementing it. Not everyone approves of the festival. -> To approve of something is to agree with it, often in an ethical, religious or moral sense. So, the preposition 'for' should be replaced with 'of' to rectify the given sentence. The correct sentence is: A good many of those doctors don't approve of unorthodox medicine. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Note: A Preposition is a word placed before a noun or a pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition) and shows a relationship in space or time or a logical relationship between two or more people, places, or things in a sentence. Examples 1) The teacher scolded the child for being late. 2) We're going to a party. · The words in bold are called prepositions because they normally take a position before (pre) a noun/pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition. · The noun/pronoun which is used with a preposition is called its Object.

questions.question: Which word in the passage is OPPOSITE in meaning to 'repulsive'?
questions.explanation: 'Repulsive' means causing a strong feeling of dislike or disgust. 'Comely' means pleasing in appearance; attractive. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Dreadful: causing great suffering, fear, or unhappiness; extremely bad or serious. Worse: of poorer quality or a lower standard; more severe or serious. Curious: eager to know or learn something; strange or unusual.

questions.question: What is Lord Henry's opinion on exhibiting the portrait?
options: He suggests sending it to the Academy.
He recommends displaying it at the Grosvenor.
He advises against exhibiting it altogether.
He proposes keeping it in private.
questions.explanation: Lord Henry expresses his opinion about exhibiting the portrait: "You must certainly send it next year to the Grosvenor." This sentence indicates that Lord Henry recommends displaying the portrait at the Grosvenor. (Paragraph 2) Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What effect did the shadows of birds in flight have on Lord Henry Wotton?
options: They reminded him of the beauty of laburnum blossoms.
They created a momentary Japanese effect.
They made him think of pallid jade-faced painters of Tokio.
They intensified the stillness of the surroundings.
questions.explanation: The shadows of birds flying by made Lord Henry think of Japanese art. This is because they created a temporary Japanese-like effect in the room. The passage says, "now and then the fantastic shadows of birds in flight flitted across the long tussore-silk curtains that were stretched in front of the huge window, producing a kind of momentary Japanese effect" (Paragraph 1). Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: "The Grosvenor is really the only place." What can be inferred from this sentence?
options: Lord Henry prefers smaller, more exclusive art exhibitions.
Lord Henry values quality over quantity in art exhibitions.
Lord Henry is critical of the Academy's selection process.
questions.explanation: Lord Henry expresses his preference for the Grosvenor art exhibition over the Academy. He says that the Academy is too big and crowded, making it hard to enjoy the artwork or see the people. Instead, he suggests that the Grosvenor is the best place to showcase artwork because it's smaller and more selective. This implies that Lord Henry values quality over quantity when it comes to art exhibitions. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the passage based upon?
options: A contemplative scene in a lavish Victorian drawing-room.
A thrilling adventure in the bustling streets of 19th-century London.
A mysterious encounter in a hidden garden of a grand estate.
An introspective moment in an artist's studio surrounded by art and beauty.
questions.explanation: In the passage, there's a room where a man named Lord Henry Wotton is lying on a couch, smoking and looking at a beautiful laburnum tree outside the window. There's also an artist named Basil Hallward in the room, admiring a portrait he painted of a very handsome young man. Lord Henry thinks the portrait is excellent and suggests Basil should exhibit it in a specific art gallery. The room is quiet, but there's a faint sound of bees and the distant noise of London. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the misspelt word from the list.
questions.explanation: Among the options, 'sesmology' is incorrectly spelt. The correct spelling is 'seismology' (the branch of science concerned with earthquakes and related phenomena). Phenomenological-relating to the science of phenomena as distinct from that of the nature of being. Unaccountable-not able to be explained or understood Irreconcilable-so different from each other that they cannot be made compatible. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: A fair judicial system is central to the functioning of a democracy. P. Courts interpret laws and ensure their uniform application. Q. Citizens rely on the judiciary to resolve disputes impartially. R. This builds public trust in legal institutions. S. Judges are expected to operate independently of political influence. 6: Judicial independence, therefore, strengthens democratic governance.
questions.explanation: The passage begins with the role of the judiciary in (1). (Q) follows by explaining citizen dependence. (S) logically describes the condition required for fairness. (P) then explains the functional role of courts. (R) shows the resulting public trust, leading to the conclusion. Sentence (6) offers a concluding insight. Thus, the correct sequence is QSPR. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. And, it took 22 years for the completion of the entire monument. B. Taj Mahal is one of the seven wonders of the world. C. He got it constructed in memory of his wife, Mumtaz Mahal. D. It is located in Agra and was constructed by Shah Jahan.
questions.explanation: The given set of sentences is about one of the seven wonders of the world, the Taj Mahal. The first sentence after rearranging will be B as it introduces the subject 'Taj Mahal'. D-C is a mandatory pair. Sentence D talks about its location and also about who got it constructed. Sentence C follows and tells for whom it was constructed. A is a concluding sentence informing about how long it took to construct it. The final arrangement will be BDCA. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding direct speech. Subham said to me, "I was watching Peaky Blinders last night".
options: Subham told me that he was watching Peaky Blinders the previous night.
Subham told me that he had been watching Peaky Blinders the previous night.
Subham told me that he has been watching Peaky Blinders the previous night.
Subham told me that he have been watching Peaky Blinders last night.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (b)Subham told me that he had been watching Peaky Blinders the previous night." Key points: · The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Past Continuous Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech will be in Past Perfect Continuous Tense. · Past Continuous (Subject + was/were+V1 + ing + Object) will be converted into Past Perfect Continuous (Subject + had been + V1 + ing + Object). · For example: o He said, "I was watching television last night" (Direct Speech) o He said that he had been watching television the previous night. (Indirect Speech) · The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Direct Speech to Indirect Speech:Direct SpeechIndirect Speech said to metold me I was watchinghe had been watching last nightthe previous night

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Although she meant her joke to be__________ the people around her took it to heart.
questions.explanation: The sentence begins with 'although', which means that the sentences joined will be contrary. The people took it to heart which they were not supposed to which means the jokes were not meant to hurt. 'Innocuous' means harmless. 'Pernicious' means 'having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way'. 'Noisome' means having an extremely offensive smell. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: What does the idiom 'Stir a hornet's nest' means?
questions.explanation: The idiom 'stir a hornet's nest' means to cause trouble or controversy. E.g. Do not raise this issue at the meeting again, as it will stir a hornet's nest. C is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate homophones to fill the blanks. The cat caught many _____ in the ____.
questions.explanation: "Mice" is the plural form of "mouse." Since the sentence talks about the cat catching something, it makes sense for the cat to catch multiple mice, hence "mice" is the correct plural form. "Aisle" refers to a passage between rows of seats, shelves, etc., especially in a church, theatre, or supermarket. It is a common location where a cat might catch mice, as they can sometimes be found scurrying along such paths. So, "The cat caught mice in the aisle" simply describes a situation where a cat caught multiple mice in an aisle. The complete sentence is "The cat caught many mice in the aisle." Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Identify the Correct Type of Adjective This majestic mountain overlooks the valley. Here, majestic is a _____ adjective.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) descriptive. "Descriptive" adjectives (also known as qualitative) provide details about the quality, appearance, or state of the noun (e.g., majestic, tall, serene). In the sentence, "majestic" describes the mountain's grandeur or impressive nature, fitting this category perfectly. (a) possessive is incorrect because possessive adjectives show ownership (e.g., "our" mountain); "majestic" does not indicate relation or belonging. (b) demonstrative is incorrect because demonstrative adjectives point to specific nouns (e.g., "that" mountain); "majestic" does not specify proximity or reference. (d) interrogative is incorrect because interrogative adjectives are used in questions to inquire about nouns (e.g., "what" mountain); "majestic" is declarative, not questioning.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate pronoun to fill in the blank. While pointing to a pile of old files, the clerk said, "________ are the documents you requested from the archives last week."
questions.explanation: The given sentence describes a clerk identifying some files for someone. The context shows the clerk is "pointing to a pile of old files," which indicates the items are plural ("documents") and likely nearby. The blank requires a pronoun that can act as the subject of the sentence and point to specific items. The correct option is "These," which is a plural demonstrative pronoun used to identify specific items that are close to the speaker. It agrees with the plural noun "documents" and the plural verb "are." The other options are incorrect. "That" and "This" are singular demonstrative pronouns and do not agree with the plural noun "documents." "It" is a singular personal pronoun and would be unsuitable for referring to multiple documents. Thus, the correct sentence is: "While pointing to a pile of old files, the clerk said, "These are the documents you requested from the archives last week."" Hence, option (d) is the answer. Note: Demonstrative pronouns: Demonstrative pronouns are used to emphasise which thing (of multiple possibilities) you are talking about. This, that, these, and those are demonstrative pronouns. Example: "These are books, I love to read." Personal Pronouns: The pronouns that are used for three persons, namely, the person speaking, the person spoken to, and the person spoken of, are called personal pronouns. The person speaking is also known as first person, and includes the following pronouns: I, me, my, mine, myself, we, our, ours, us, ourselves. The person spoken to is also known as second person, and includes the following pronouns: You, your, yours, yourself, yourselves. The person spoken of is also known as third person, and includes the following pronouns: He, his, him, himself, she, her, hers, herself, it, its, itself, they, their, theirs, them, themselves. In some cases we tend to refer to nouns that are inanimate or are in neuter gender through pronouns; these are known as impersonal pronouns and include the following: it, they, them, its, theirs.

questions.question: Change the following sentence from active to passive voice. The diligent research team is currently analysing a vast amount of complex data to uncover significant patterns for their breakthrough study.
options: A vast amount of complex data was being analysed by the diligent research team to uncover significant patterns for their study.
A vast amount of complex data were currently being analysed by the diligent research team to uncover significant patterns for their study.
A vast amount of complex data is currently being analysed by the diligent research team to uncover significant patterns for their breakthrough study.
A vast amount of complex data is analysed by the diligent research team to uncover significant patterns for their breakthrough study.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb ['being' in the given sentence] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Present Continuous. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Present Continuous. The format of Active voice is: Subject + am/is/are + V1 + ing + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + am/is/are + being + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: Arnab is typing a document. (Active Voice) A document is being typed by Arnab. (Passive Voice) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c). Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate synonym of 'Upfront':
questions.explanation: Dissembling: conceal or disguise one's true feelings or beliefs Upfront: free in expressing oneʼs true feelings and opinions Ingenuous: showing simplicity and candidness Equivocal: without a single clear or definite meaning or intention Ambiguous: having more than one possible meaning Thus, 'ingenuous' is most similar in meaning to the given word. B is the right answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct synonym of "INTRICATE":
questions.explanation: 'Intricate' - very complicated or detailed. 'Complicated' - complex. 'Honest' - truthful and sincere; 'straightforward' - uncomplicated and easy to do or understand; 'sincere' - free from pretence or deceit. Thus, 'complicated' is the only word that can be a synonym of the word 'intricate'. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The station master commanded the staff to clear the platform immediately as the express train was scheduled to arrive on that track.
options: The station master said to the staff, "Clear the platform immediately as the express train is scheduled to arrive on this track."
The station master said to the staff, "You should clear the platform immediately as the express train is scheduled to arrive on this track."
The station master commanded, "To clear the platform immediately as the express train was scheduled to arrive on that track."
The station master said to the staff, "The platform must be cleared immediately as the express train is scheduled to arrive."
questions.explanation: Imperative Sentences in Direct Speech are used to demonstrate a command. The reporting verb "commanded" is changed to "said to". In indirect speech, "To" is used before the Principal Verb, which is removed in direct speech to form the base imperative. For example: John ordered his assistant to bring him a glass of water. (Indirect Speech) John said to his assistant, "Bring me a glass of water." (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: · 'Commanded' is changed to 'said to'. · The infinitive 'to clear' is changed to the imperative 'Clear'. · The verb 'was scheduled' (Past Simple) is changed to 'is scheduled' (Present Simple). · 'That track' is changed to 'this track'. · Inverted commas ("..") are placed around the direct command. Therefore, the correct answer is: The station master said to the staff, "Clear the platform immediately as the express train is scheduled to arrive on this track." Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the one which can be substituted for the given sentence. Showing great attention to details and correct behaviour
questions.explanation: WORDMEANING Zealoushaving or showing passion. Punctualhappening or doing something at the agreed or proper time. Punctiliousshowing great attention to detail or correct behaviour. Fanatica person filled with excessive and single-minded zeal, especially for an extreme religious or political cause. So, 'punctilious' is the right word here. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Substitute the underlined word-segment with most appropriate idiom. The manager had to make a difficult and unpleasant decision to downsize the team for the company's survival.
questions.explanation: The phrase "make a difficult and unpleasant decision" refers to facing a tough situation and accepting it, often with courage because it's unavoidable. The idiom "bite the bullet" means to endure a painful or difficult situation with courage and stoicism. This fits the context of the manager having to make a hard, unpleasant choice for the company's sake. Therefore, it is the most suitable idiomatic replacement. a) spill the beans - to reveal secret information. (Incorrect meaning; the manager isn't revealing a secret but making a tough choice.) c) break the ice - to ease tension or initiate conversation in a social setting. (Unrelated to making a difficult decision.) d) hit the sack - to go to bed or go to sleep. (Unrelated to the professional context of decision-making.) Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Change the following sentence from passive to active voice. By whom was this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies directed and produced last year?
options: Whom directed and produced this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies last year?
Who has directed and produced this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies last year?
Who directed and produced this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies last year?
Who was directing and producing this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies last year?
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. "By whom?" in a passive sentence is changed into "Who" while transforming from Passive to Active. The tense of the sentence (Past Indefinite) remains the same. The Passive voice in this question is in Past Indefinite. So, the sentence in the Active voice should be in Past Indefinite. For example: By whom were you taught Math? (Passive) Who taught you Math? (Active) The options except (c) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (c): Who directed and produced this beautiful, award-winning documentary about the migration of monarch butterflies last year? Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Identify the misspelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling and meaning of the correct answer is: Heinous: very bad and shocking. Thus, the correct answer is option (b). Shimmering: to shine with a soft light that seems to be moving.
